hero.name: Sahir Hameed
hero.narrative: Working to improve myself every day.

about.title: My Story
about.subtitle: The journey so far
bio: I'm a Computer Science student at the University of Texas at Austin with a Minor in Business, currently working as a Data & AI Engineer at Lockheed Martin — building systems that actually matter for mission-critical defense infrastructure.

From architecting document automation systems to building Chrome extensions that think and training models to predict the unpredictable — every project taught me something new about turning complexity into clarity.
When I'm not coding, you'll find me working out, watching anime, or spending time with family and friends. I believe the best solutions come from curious minds that never stop exploring.

highlights.label: Education
highlights.value: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science, Minor in Business — UT Austin

items.title: Data & AI Engineer
description: Architecting a full-stack document lifecycle automation system using C# .NET, Python, and GitLab, implementing automated email notifications, archival workflows, and version-controlled repository storage for enterprise document management
Designed and deployed RESTful API integrations connecting SAP BusinessObjects WebI with GitLab repositories, enabling automated data extraction and migration for compliance and long-term storage
Developed Python automation scripts for data pipeline operations, backup procedures, and system integrations, reducing manual overhead by 20% and improving operational efficiency
Optimized SQL Server queries and restructured database schemas, improving application performance by 30% and reducing load times for 100+ enterprise users
Collaborated in Agile/Scrum teams to gather requirements, troubleshoot system issues, and deliver scalable internal web applications with continuous integration practices

items.title: Undergraduate Research Assistant
description: Designed high-performance client/server architecture using CXL shared memory for distributed systems, implementing infrastructure as code for repeatable deployments
Developed multithreaded C++ applications with object-oriented design patterns, optimizing resource management and transaction retry policies

items.title: Software Engineering Intern
description: Designed and deployed an API development project for an inventory management system using Excel's VBA and JavaScript, improving visibility across 100+ hospitals in an Agile/DevOps environment.
Implemented CI/CD pipeline enhancements and workflow optimizations, reducing manual errors by 30% and saving users 15 hours/week

items.name: Seblea
items.description: College sublease platform enabling students to find and list subleases with real-time listings, secure authentication, and a streamlined search experience built for the UT Austin community.

items.name: Video Game Sales Prediction
items.description: Collaborated in an Agile team of 4 to build and optimize a predictive model, improving R² accuracy by 83% through hyperparameter tuning, iterative testing, and data-driven evaluation metrics. Integrated third-party APIs for data retrieval and implemented automated data processing pipelines.

items.name: Web Page Summarizer
items.description: Developed Chrome extension utilizing RESTful API integration with OpenAI's GPT-3.5 Turbo for real-time web content summarization. Applied prompt engineering and LangChain framework to optimize API performance and response accuracy.

items.name: Personal Portfolio
items.description: Terminal-themed portfolio website with custom chatbot, command palette (Cmd+K), scroll animations, and a fully JSON-driven content system for easy updates.
